commit.message:
Infrastructure for figures and math database (#15)

* Initial plan

* Add infrastructure for figures and math database

- Create inst/terms/ directory with YAML term definition files for common
  ergm terms (edges, triangle, mutual, nodematch)
- Add R/term_db.R with functions for reading YAML term data, parsing
  edgelist notation, drawing network figures (via netplot), and caching
  figures using MD5 hashes
- Modify .add_term_metadata() in R/parse_ergm.R to overlay YAML data
  (math and figure) onto the ergm metadata lookup pipeline
- Pass network directedness from parse_ergm_model() to YAML lookup
- Add yaml to Imports and netplot to Suggests in DESCRIPTION
- Add comprehensive tests in inst/tinytest/test_term_db.R (40 tests)

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use named column indexing in .draw_with_netplot for robustness

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments: fix hash usage, add layout to YAML, move netplot/network to Imports, refactor tests

- Fix .draw_with_netplot to write directly to the hash-based cache path
  instead of a random tempfile (addresses review on term_db.R:172)
- Add layout (x/y coordinates) to all YAML term files and pass through
  to netplot::nplot (addresses review on edges.directed.yml:2)
- Handle degenerate layouts (identical coordinates) with small perturbation
- Fix nplot grid object rendering by explicitly calling print()
- Quote 'y' key in YAML files to avoid YAML 1.1 boolean parsing
- Refactor YAML file lookup tests to use a for-loop (addresses review
  on test_term_db.R:27-48)
- Move netplot and network from Suggests to Imports (addresses review
  on test_term_db.R:93); yaml already in Imports
- Remove requireNamespace guards for netplot/network (now hard deps)

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Quote both x and y keys in YAML layout for consistency

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address round 2 review: plotfun API, YAML format, comma edgelist, README

- Add tabulergm_default_plotfun(), tabulergm_set_plotfun(),
  tabulergm_get_plotfun() for flexible plot function swapping
- Switch YAML layout to inline arrays (x: [0, 1], y: [0, 0])
  and use yaml::read_yaml handlers to prevent YAML 1.1 boolean
  coercion of 'y' keys
- Extend .parse_plot_edgelist() to support comma-separated segments
  (e.g. "0->1, 2->1") in addition to chain format
- Remove engine parameter in favour of plotfun architecture
- Update AGENTS.md to require `quarto render README.qmd`
- Re-render README.md via quarto

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add explanatory comments for YAML boolean handler workaround

Co-authored-by: gvegayon <893619+gvegayon@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
tdarr: fix CPU oversubscription with cpus limit and thread docs

libx265 defaults to using all CPU threads per process, so 4 workers
on a 16-core machine spawns 64 threads competing for 16 cores.

- Add cpus: 12 to tdarr service in compose.starr.yml
- Document pools=4 x265-params fix for per-encoder thread limiting
- Add SDR branch custom arguments node (Node 6b) for thread control
- Update HDR branch x265-params to include pools=4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add rounded shore masking and inner-corner shore pieces for water tiles (#299)

* Initial plan

* feat: add rounded shore masking and corner shore pieces

- Add overflow: hidden to .cell so border-radius clips shore overlays
- Create assets/shore-corner.svg: quarter-circle radial-gradient shore piece
- Add CSS for .shore-corner-overlay matching .shore-overlay layout
- Extend _addShoreOverlays() to render shore-corner-overlay for each
  L-shaped pair of adjacent water neighbours (4 corner orientations)
- Extract SHORE_DIRECTIONS and SHORE_CORNERS as module-level constants
- Add 8 new tests covering corner overlay counts, rotation angles,
  src reference, and independence from straight shore counts

Co-authored-by: AvinZarlez <4392252+AvinZarlez@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: skip corner shore when diagonal tile is also water

A corner piece is only needed to fill a visible gap at the inner corner
of a water L-shape. When the diagonal tile in that corner direction is
also water, the corner is interior to the water body and no gap exists.

Add `diag` offset to each SHORE_CORNERS entry and skip the corner when
`grid.getTile(row + diag.dRow, col + diag.dCol) === 'water'`.

This prevents the 4-quarter-circle "island" pattern that appeared in
the centre of 2×2 (and larger rectangular) water blocks, and suppresses
corners on all interior tiles of any contiguous water shape.

Tests: update cross/plus description; add 2×2 (0 corners) and 3×3
interior tile (0 corners) test cases.

Co-authored-by: AvinZarlez <4392252+AvinZarlez@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: AvinZarlez <4392252+AvinZarlez@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add config to allow disabling dag versioning (#2470)

Adds a Cosmos setting to disable DAG versioning (dbt project hash in
`doc_md`) to improve DAG parsing performance, especially on Airflow 2.x.

New `airflow.cfg` setting: `enable_dag_versioning` in the `[cosmos]`
section (default: `True`).
When False, Cosmos skips computing the dbt project hash and does not
update dag.doc_md. Parsing is faster; on Airflow 3, DAG versioning will
no longer reflect dbt project changes.

## Changes
- `cosmos/settings.py`: Added `enable_dag_versioning =
conf.getboolean("cosmos", "enable_dag_versioning", fallback=True)`.
- `cosmos/converter.py`: In `_add_dbt_project_hash_to_dag_docs`, return
early when `settings.enable_dag_versioning` is `False`.
- `tests/test_converter.py`: Added
`test_dag_versioning_skipped_when_setting_disabled` to assert the hash
is not computed and `doc_md` is unchanged when the setting is disabled.
- `docs/reference/configs/cosmos-conf.rst`: Documented
`enable_dag_versioning` (default, env var, behaviour when disabled).

## Usage
No DAG code changes. Configure once via `airflow.cfg` or with the
corresponding environment variable:

**airflow.cfg:**
```
[cosmos]
enable_dag_versioning = False
```
or

**Environment variable:**
```
export AIRFLOW__COSMOS__ENABLE_DAG_VERSIONING=false
```

closes: #2386

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: WebAuthn registration for iOS passkeys (Face ID)

- Set ResidentKey to 'preferred' for discoverable credential support
  (required for iOS Passwords/Keychain passkey storage)
- Auto-detect request origin and add to RPOrigins dynamically so
  the origin validation passes when accessing from mobile devices
- Support comma-separated WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN for multiple origins
- Pre-read request body to prevent double-consumption issue
- Include transports and authenticatorAttachment in attestation response
- Add structured error logging for WebAuthn ceremonies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add minimal wp-env PHPUnit application test scaffolding and developer-oriented docs (#178)

* Initial plan

* feat: add wp-env phpunit bootstrap docs and ci snippet

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: address review feedback on phpunit bootstrap docs

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: restructure testing guide with starlight steps and first test flow

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: rewrite testing guide as developer-oriented authoring walkthrough

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: tighten test examples in application testing guide

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden phpunit setup and activate CI test job

- Activate test-application CI job (was commented out)
- Remove deprecated phpunit.xml.dist attributes
- Add tests/php/ExampleTest.php as working smoke test
- Use container phpunit directly in test:php script (no Composer wrapper)
- Restore yoast/phpunit-polyfills as explicit dep (WordPress test bootstrap requirement)
- Remove phpunit/phpunit and wp-phpunit/wp-phpunit (provided by container and polyfills)
- Simplify bootstrap.php: drop non-wp-env fallback, fail fast with clear message
- Update testing docs: wp-env-exclusive constraint, tests-cli rationale, configuration notes

* ci: run application tests after static analysis passes

Exit early benefit: avoid spinning up Docker/wp-env when PHPStan
or lint already failed. Cache keys still resolve via needs: setup.

* docs: update github-actions doc with test-application job and stage structure

commit.message:
feat: LightRAG-inspired Knowledge Graph + dual-level retrieval

Major RAG pipeline improvements inspired by LightRAG architecture:

Phase 1 — Infrastructure:
- Token budget system: precise token counting replaces char-based limits
  (MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS, MAX_ENTITY_TOKENS, MAX_RELATION_TOKENS, MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS)
- LLM keyword extraction: HIGH_LEVEL (concepts) + LOW_LEVEL (entities)
  with 5-min TTL cache, heuristic fallback when LLM unavailable
- Vector space versioning: embeddingModelId column on chunks, entities,
  relations for detecting stale vectors after model switch

Phase 2 — Knowledge Graph:
- New schema: kg_entities + kg_relations tables with pgvector embeddings
- Entity/relation extraction via LLM from document chunks (batch processing)
- Description merging + LLM summarization when >8 mentions or >1200 tokens
- Entity name normalization (CJK-aware), N-ary → binary decomposition

Phase 3 — Dual-level retrieval + query routing:
- Entity vector search (LOW_LEVEL keywords → entity embeddings)
- Relation vector search (HIGH_LEVEL keywords → relation embeddings)
- Graph expansion: entity hits → traverse relations → related entities
- KG context prepended to chunk context in buildContextFromSections()
- Unified token budget: entities + relations + chunks within limits

Phase 4 — Cascade updates & health:
- KG construction fires after document embedding (non-blocking)
- Cascade cleanup on document deletion/re-embedding
- Embedding health check utility for detecting stale vectors

New files: lib/kg/{query-keywords,entity-extraction,entity-merging,
kg-pipeline,kg-retrieval,embedding-health}.ts, lib/schema-kg.ts
Migrations: 0018_knowledge_graph.sql, 0019_embedding_model_tracking.sql

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(backend): switch base image Alpine→Debian bookworm-slim

Alpine uses musl libc; packages like orjson, vtracer, cryptography only
ship manylinux (glibc) wheels and cannot install on Alpine without the
full Rust toolchain, causing uv sync to fail with exit code 1.

Changes:
- Base image: ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.11-alpine → bookworm-slim
- Replace apk with apt-get + Debian equivalents
- Add libpq-dev for psycopg[binary]
- Remove --quiet from uv sync to surface errors in build logs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Claude/split design docs 2 vgjc (#11)

* Add codon palindrome hairpin hypothesis and empirical validation plan to DESIGN.md

Section 2.7 formalises the observation that complement palindrome codons
(x, θ(x), x) in a transition sequence are hairpin turns in concept space —
signatures of dialectical or hedged language that visits the semantic antipode
and returns. Derives the null baseline (ρ_hp = 1/9 for random sequences),
states the three-class probe prediction (Dialectical > Direct ≈ null > Negated),
and adds the antonym-retrieval corollary.

Section 3 (Empirical Validation) defines the four-phase test programme:
Phase 1 random-text null distribution, Phase 2 structured code corpus,
Phase 3 matryoshka/embedding models, Phase 4 standard local LLMs.
Each phase carries the palindrome sub-test so the hairpin hypothesis is
evaluated across the full performance ladder.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ENBnY3M7Wzbvi5DZkhRMhy

* Split monolithic DESIGN.md into DESIGN, PREDICTIONS, and TESTING

DESIGN.md: mathematical framework only — Z₄ algebra, Gray map, Lee metric,
complement involution, run-reduction, 64-bit key, block files, window queries.
Renumbered §1–§3 (was §0–§2). Persuasive prose replaced with explanatory prose
throughout (per drafting rule: prose explains the math, not persuades).

PREDICTIONS.md: nine falsifiable predictions derived from the framework plus the
canonical CGAT→Z₄ mapping. Includes: Gray code = purine/pyrimidine × keto/amino
axes (algebraic result, no test needed); hairpin density ordering; CpG suppression
analog; transition/transversion weighted Lee metric; reverse-complement antonym
retrieval; two-stage PAM/seed search architecture; document secondary structure;
codon usage bias; Z₄ alphabet optimality via Kerdock/Preparata. Each prediction
states a falsification condition and a test-phase reference.

TESTING.md: four-phase experimental programme (T1 random null, T2 code corpus,
T3 embedding models, T4 local LLMs). Each phase carries a core retrieval
benchmark and prediction sub-tests keyed to PREDICTIONS.md. Cross-phase matrix
shows which phase tests which prediction.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ENBnY3M7Wzbvi5DZkhRMhy

* Potential fix for pull request finding
